video_title: Castlevania: Nocturne Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
this shit sucks. I thought maybe they would address some of the issues with season 1 but no, it all sucks. what a major disappointment compared to the original series.

first and foremost, every character relationship feels cheap and unearned. either they have no chemistry because the dialogue is poorly written, or I don't learn about a character's backstory or motivations until after they DIE (it's happened twice now), so why should I care about the impact that death has on another character if the setup and payoff are backwards. for richter, it's him to annette. they're supposed to have a budding relationship but I genuinely felt none of that blooming in s1. and in s2, the dialogue they share is witless so their interactions fall flat and uninteresting, unlike trevor and sypha. we don't learn anything about annette or why she cares about edouard until AFTER he dies, so his death holds no weight. olrox and mizrak's romance is the weirdest development, because mizrak is a devout christian knight that detests vampires by nature of his faith, and olrox is an immortal working for the villain but with ambiguous personal motives, yet we're still given no insight as to why he creeps around protecting mizrak other than their secret love affair is taboo so they get off to it. not to mention they also share zero chemistry. the band of protagonists have little chemistry between the lot of themselves too, so I find no enjoyment in watching them interact as a group, unlike trevor, sypha and alucard.

then there's plot and pacing. it is a mess to say the least. most scenes are just open-ended melodrama that loses me through bloated exposition fluff, or lack there when it's needed most. sometimes the story will pose a thematic question or character choice but then not explore that conversation, it's jarring when I don't know where the story wants to point my attention. every character interaction, every dialogue, every monologue, wants to be so profound and poetic, but because the lines are so bluntly written and many of the VAs performances lack any vibrato or emotion, there is no depth or tension or dynamic range- the star wars prequels effect in summary. and everyone is always crying all the damn time, and when something is done all the time, the times when it should matter don't. plot contrivances are a dime a dozen too. annette randomly runs into her former vampire slave owner out in the open very close to dawn, juste avoids richter his whole adolescence because?? and then richter regains his connection to magic because he gets angry enough?? and is then masterfully proficient with it despite not practicing it since he was like 10. why can maria's mother freely roam about after she's been turned if she was turned to be erzebet's twisted slave? but no it has no impact on the plot despite being a huge deal in the s1 finale, she poses no threat to the heroes so what was the point. also, they just completely retconned how night creatures work??? it holds no continuity from the rules of the world established in the original series. night creatures are given souls from hell at random to inhabit corpses, the dead aren't reborn inside they're own body, because that would imply ALL souls go to hell when they die.

don't even get me started on erzebet. bland, run of the mill genocidal psychopath who wants to rule the world. nothing about her is appealing because she's just so boring.

video_title: Castlevania: Nocturne Season 2 | Official Trailer | Netflix
comment_text:
The second season was much better than the first, even if some of the art style was inconsistent.  The designs for the night creatures were awesome (though this was from the first season as well), and Richter and Annette's fighting styles were fantastic.  It seemed that the animation for Alucard's fights was very inconsistent though, particularly when he was jobbing.  Comparing his fight in episode 1 to episode 4 is rather stark.

I still think it would have made more sense to name Annette 'Terra' (or Tiera) and have Terra be 'Annette'.  Then change Annette back into Maria's sister.  Separate from any of that, I didn't feel they gave Annette and Richter the chemistry that they ought to have in order for the relationship to not feel forced.  Also the alliance between the revolution and Alucard felt forced and largely out of character for him.  And Juste and Maria felt forced and off tone.  A lot of odd pairings that didn't really feel like the chemistry was there, whether the relationship was supposed to be antagonistic or allied.  Probably the best was Drolta and Erzsebet.

Definitely missing Dracula though.  And Shaft.  Maybe if there is a season 3?

comment_text: It looks good. I'm pumped about Juste and Alucard, but did you really have to race swap Richter's girl? None of the successor Belmonts are black. You can make a new black Belmont, I guess, but that's not how the games go. Julius Belmont is still light skinned. Take the wokeness down a notch, you have plenty of other characters of color that never existed in the games and they work great for the narrative, let the existing legacy stand out of respect for the source material...please? It's not about having more white people, it's me wanting to see an adaptation of a legacy franchise when you're just trying to pander to general audiences. That's not Castlevania. It's about grinding alone through a castle...hm, I guess these haven't been faithful adaptations at all.
